steps.title: Check current kyverno deployment
steps.description:
Verify your kyverno version and configuration:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n kyverno -l app.kubernetes.io/name=kyverno
helm list -n kyverno 2>/dev/null || echo "Not installed via Helm"
```
This feature requires a working kyverno installation.

steps.title: Review kyverno configuration
steps.description:
Inspect the relevant kyverno configuration:
```bash
kubectl get all -n kyverno -l app.kubernetes.io/name=kyverno
kubectl get configmap -n kyverno -l app.kubernetes.io/part-of=kyverno
```
### Problem Statement

I'd like Kyverno to be able to log in JSON. This allows log aggregation pipelines to be naive to the format of incoming logs.

### Solution Description

A CLI argument that makes Kyverno log in JSON instead of klog.

steps.title: Apply the fix for [Feature] JSON Logging
steps.description:
*: If this PR results in new or altered behavior which is user facing, you

read and follow the steps outlined in the [PR documentation guide](pr_documentation.md) and add Proof Manifests as defined below.
-->

### Proof Manifests
```yaml
apiVersion: v1
kind: ConfigMap
metadata:
  name: roles-dictionary
  namespace: default
data:
  allowed-roles: "[\"cluster-admin\", \"cluster-operator\", \"tenant-admin\"]"
```

steps.title: Verify the feature works
steps.description:
Test that the new capability is working as expected:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n kyverno -l app.kubernetes.io/name=kyverno
kubectl get events -n kyverno --sort-by='.lastTimestamp' | tail -10
```
Confirm the feature described in "[Feature] JSON Logging" is functioning correctly.

resolution.summary:
*: If this PR results in new or altered behavior which is user facing, you

read and follow the steps outlined in the [PR documentation guide](pr_documentation.md) and add Proof Manifests as defined below.
-->

### Proof Manifests
codeSnippets:
apiVersion: v1
kind: ConfigMap
metadata:
  name: roles-dictionary
  namespace: default
data:
  allowed-roles: "[\"cluster-admin\", \"cluster-operator\", \"tenant-admin\"]"

name: prepend-image-registry
policies:
  - prepend_image_registry.yaml
resources:
  - resource.yaml
variables: values.yaml
results:
  - policy: prepend-registry
    rule: prepend-registry-containers
    resource: mypod
    # if mutate rule
    patchedResource: patchedResource01.yaml
    kind: Pod
    result: pass

prerequisites.description: A running Kubernetes cluster with kyverno installed or the issue environment reproducible.
